645: | `bdg --list`              | List all CDP domains (53) | `bdg --list`                            |
646: | `bdg --describe <domain>` | Show domain methods       | `bdg --describe Page`                   |
647: | `bdg --describe <method>` | Show method signature     | `bdg --describe Page.captureScreenshot` |
648: | `bdg --search <term>`     | Search CDP methods        | `bdg --search screenshot`               |
649: 
650: **Raw CDP Commands**
651: 
652: | Command                     | Description         | Example                                                     |
653: | --------------------------- | ------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------- |
654: | `bdg cdp <method>`          | Execute CDP method  | `bdg cdp Page.reload 2>&1`                                  |
655: | `bdg cdp <method> '<json>'` | CDP with parameters | `bdg cdp Network.setCookie '{"name":"x","value":"y"}' 2>&1` |
656: 
657: **Error Handling Pattern**
658: 
659: Always use `2>&1` to capture stderr for error handling:
660: 
661: ```bash
662: #!/bin/bash
663: trap "bdg stop 2>&1" EXIT INT TERM
664: command -v bdg || { echo "Install bdg first"; exit 1; }
665: bdg "$URL" 2>&1 || exit 1
666: # ... operations ...
667: # Cleanup runs automatically on exit
668: ```
669: 
670: ---
671: 
672: ## 7. FEATURES
673: 
674: | Feature            | CLI (bdg)                                   | MCP (Code Mode)                         |
675: | ------------------ | ------------------------------------------- | --------------------------------------- |
676: | **Installation**   | `npm install -g browser-debugger-cli@alpha` | Code Mode + UTCP config                 |
677: | **Token Cost**     | Lowest                                      | Medium                                  |
678: | **CDP Access**     | All 644 methods                             | MCP-exposed subset (26 tools)           |
679: | **Self-Discovery** | `--list`, `--describe`, `--search`          | `search_tools()`                        |
680: | **Best For**       | Quick debugging, inspection                 | Multi-tool workflows, parallel browsers |
681: 
682: **Core capabilities available through both approaches:**
683: - Screenshot capture
684: - DOM inspection and querying
685: - Console log access
686: - Network monitoring and HAR export
687: - CSS debugging
688: - JavaScript execution
689: - Cookie management
690: - Full Chrome DevTools Protocol access
691: 
692: ---
693: 
694: ## 8. EXAMPLES
695: 
696: ### Example 1: Full Debug Session
697: 
698: ```bash
699: #!/bin/bash
700: # Complete debug session with error handling
701: trap "bdg stop 2>&1" EXIT INT TERM
702: 
703: # Verify CLI is available
704: command -v bdg || { echo "Install: npm install -g browser-debugger-cli@alpha"; exit 1; }
705: 
706: # Start session
707: bdg https://example.com 2>&1 || exit 1
708: 
709: # Capture initial state
710: bdg screenshot before.png 2>&1
711: bdg console logs 2>&1 > console-before.json
712: 
713: # Enable network monitoring
714: bdg cdp Network.enable 2>&1
715: sleep 3
716: 
717: # Export network trace
718: bdg har export session.har 2>&1
719: bdg screenshot after.png 2>&1
720: 
721: echo "Debug session complete. Files: before.png, after.png, session.har, console-before.json"
722: ```
723: 
724: ### Example 2: JavaScript Execution
725: 
726: ```bash
727: bdg https://example.com 2>&1
728: 
729: # Get page title
730: bdg js "document.title" 2>&1
731: 
732: # Count links on page
733: bdg js "document.querySelectorAll('a').length" 2>&1
734: 
735: # Get all link hrefs
736: bdg js "Array.from(document.querySelectorAll('a')).map(a => a.href)" 2>&1
737: 
738: bdg stop 2>&1
739: ```
740: 
741: ### Example 3: DOM Query and Interaction
742: 
743: ```bash
744: bdg https://example.com 2>&1
745: 
746: # Query elements by class
747: bdg dom query ".nav-link" 2>&1
748: 
749: # Click a button via JavaScript
750: bdg js "document.querySelector('#submit-btn').click()" 2>&1
751: 
752: # Check result after click
753: bdg screenshot post-click.png 2>&1
754: bdg console logs 2>&1
755: 
756: bdg stop 2>&1
757: ```
758: 
759: ### Example 4: Network Analysis
760: 
761: ```bash
762: bdg https://example.com 2>&1
763: bdg cdp Network.enable 2>&1
764: 
765: # Wait for full page load activity
766: sleep 5
767: bdg har export network-trace.har 2>&1
768: bdg stop 2>&1
769: 
770: # Find slow requests (over 1 second)
771: jq '.log.entries[] | select(.time > 1000) | {url: .request.url, time}' network-trace.har
772: 
773: # Find failed requests
774: jq '.log.entries[] | select(.response.status >= 400) | {url: .request.url, status: .response.status}' network-trace.har
775: ```
776: 
777: ### Example 5: Parallel Comparison (MCP)
778: 
779: ```typescript
780: // Compare production and staging side by side
781: call_tool_chain(`
782:   await chrome_devtools_1.chrome_devtools_1_navigate_page({
783:     url: "https://production.example.com"
784:   });
785:   await chrome_devtools_2.chrome_devtools_2_navigate_page({
786:     url: "https://staging.example.com"
787:   });
788: 
789:   const prodScreenshot = await chrome_devtools_1.chrome_devtools_1_take_screenshot({});
790:   const stagingScreenshot = await chrome_devtools_2.chrome_devtools_2_take_screenshot({});
791:   const prodLogs = await chrome_devtools_1.chrome_devtools_1_list_console_messages({});
792:   const stagingLogs = await chrome_devtools_2.chrome_devtools_2_list_console_messages({});
793: 
794:   return {
795:     production: { screenshot: prodScreenshot, logs: prodLogs },
796:     staging: { screenshot: stagingScreenshot, logs: stagingLogs }
797:   };
798: `)
799: ```
800: 
801: ### Example 6: Cookie Session Management
802: 
803: ```bash
804: bdg https://example.com 2>&1
805: bdg cdp Network.enable 2>&1
806: 
807: # Export current cookies
808: bdg network cookies 2>&1 > cookies.json
809: 
810: # Inject an auth token
811: bdg cdp Network.setCookie '{
812:   "name": "auth_token",
813:   "value": "test-session-token",
814:   "domain": "example.com",
815:   "path": "/",
816:   "secure": true,
817:   "httpOnly": true
818: }' 2>&1
819: 
820: # Reload to test authenticated state
821: bdg cdp Page.reload 2>&1
822: bdg screenshot authenticated.png 2>&1
823: 
824: bdg stop 2>&1
825: ```
826: 
827: ---
828: 
829: ## 9. TROUBLESHOOTING
830: 
831: ### Error/Cause/Fix Reference
832: 
833: | Error | Cause | Fix |
834: | ----- | ----- | --- |
835: | `command not found: bdg` | CLI not installed or not in PATH | Run `npm install -g browser-debugger-cli@alpha`. Check PATH with `npm config get prefix` |
836: | `Error: Could not find Chrome` | Chrome path not set or binary missing | Set `CHROME_PATH` env var. See path examples below |
837: | `Error: No active session` | Session not started or crashed | Run `bdg stop 2>&1` then retry `bdg <url> 2>&1` |
838: | `Failed to move to new namespace` | Linux sandbox restriction | Enable user namespaces or set `CHROME_FLAGS`. See Linux sandbox section |
839: | `bdg fails on Windows` | Native Windows not supported | Install WSL with `wsl --install`, then install bdg inside WSL |
840: | Chrome DevTools tools missing in Code Mode | UTCP config missing or disabled | Check `.utcp_config.json`, confirm `disabled: false`, restart your session |
841: | MCP server fails to start | Port conflict or config error | Run `lsof -i :9222` and kill conflicting processes |
842: | `EADDRINUSE: address already in use :::9222` | Port 9222 already taken | Run `kill -9 $(lsof -t -i :9222)` to free the port |
843: 
844: ### CLI: Command Not Found
845: 
846: ```bash
847: # Install bdg
848: npm install -g browser-debugger-cli@alpha
849: 
850: # Or fix PATH
851: npm config get prefix
852: export PATH="$(npm config get prefix)/bin:$PATH"
853: 
854: # Verify
855: command -v bdg
856: ```
857: 
858: ### CLI: Chrome Not Found
859: 
860: ```bash
861: # macOS
862: export CHROME_PATH="/Applications/Google Chrome.app/Contents/MacOS/Google Chrome"
863: 
864: # Linux
865: export CHROME_PATH="/usr/bin/google-chrome"
866: 
867: # Confirm Chrome exists at the path
868: ls -la "$CHROME_PATH"
869: 
870: # Add to your shell profile
871: echo 'export CHROME_PATH="/path/to/chrome"' >> ~/.zshrc
872: ```
873: 
874: ### CLI: Session Won't Start
875: 
876: ```bash
877: # Force stop any existing sessions
878: bdg stop 2>&1
879: 
880: # Find zombie Chrome processes
881: ps aux | grep -i chrome | grep -i debug
882: 
883: # Kill zombie processes
884: pkill -f "chrome.*remote-debugging"
885: 
886: # Retry
887: bdg https://example.com 2>&1
888: ```
889: 
890: ### CLI: Sandbox Errors (Linux)
891: 
892: Fix in order of preference:
893: 
894: ```bash
895: # Option 1: Enable user namespaces (preferred)
896: sudo sysctl -w kernel.unprivileged_userns_clone=1
897: 
898: # Option 2: Disable setuid sandbox only
899: export CHROME_FLAGS="--disable-setuid-sandbox"
900: 
901: # Option 3: No sandbox (least secure, last resort)
902: export CHROME_FLAGS="--no-sandbox"
903: ```
904: 
905: ### CLI: Windows Not Supported
906: 
907: ```bash
908: # Install WSL
909: wsl --install
910: 
911: # Inside WSL, install bdg
912: npm install -g browser-debugger-cli@alpha
913: ```
914: 
915: ### MCP: Tool Not Found
916: 
917: 1. Open `.utcp_config.json` and confirm the chrome_devtools entry exists
918: 2. Check that `disabled` is not set to `true`
919: 3. Restart your Claude Code or OpenCode session
920: 4. Run `list_tools()` and look for chrome_devtools_* entries
921: 
922: ### MCP: Connection Failed
923: 
924: ```bash
925: # Test the MCP server directly
926: npx chrome-devtools-mcp@latest --version
927: 
928: # Check for port conflicts
929: lsof -i :9222
930: 
931: # Kill any conflicting processes
932: kill $(lsof -t -i :9222)
933: ```
934: 
935: ### MCP: Port Already in Use
936: 
937: ```bash
938: # Find the process using port 9222
939: lsof -i :9222
940: 
941: # Kill it
942: kill -9 $(lsof -t -i :9222)
943: 
944: # Confirm the port is free
945: lsof -i :9222
946: # Should return nothing
947: ```
948: 
949: ---
950: 
951: ## 10. RESOURCES
952: 
953: ### Related Documentation
954: 
955: | Document        | Location                                                                  | Purpose              |
956: | --------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------- | -------------------- |
957: | SKILL.md        | `.opencode/skills/mcp-chrome-devtools/SKILL.md`                      | Complete workflows   |
958: | CDP Patterns    | `.opencode/skills/mcp-chrome-devtools/references/cdp_patterns.md`    | Domain patterns      |
959: | Troubleshooting | `.opencode/skills/mcp-chrome-devtools/references/troubleshooting.md` | Detailed fixes       |
960: | Examples        | `.opencode/skills/mcp-chrome-devtools/examples/README.md`            | Production templates |
961: | Code Mode Guide | `.opencode/skills/mcp-code-mode/INSTALL_GUIDE.md`                          | Code Mode setup      |
962: 
963: ### Configuration Paths
964: 
965: | Client  | Configuration                 | Purpose                      |
966: | ------- | ----------------------------- | ---------------------------- |
967: | **CLI** | `CHROME_PATH` env var         | Browser location             |
968: | **MCP** | `.mcp.json` / `opencode.json` | Code Mode server             |
969: | **MCP** | `.utcp_config.json`           | Chrome DevTools registration |
970: 
971: ### MCP Tools Reference (26 tools)
972: 
973: | Tool                    | Purpose            | CLI Equivalent                                   |
974: | ----------------------- | ------------------ | ------------------------------------------------ |
975: | `navigate_page`         | Navigate to URL    | `bdg <url>`                                      |
976: | `take_screenshot`       | Capture screenshot | `bdg screenshot`                                 |
977: | `list_console_messages` | Get console logs   | `bdg console logs`                               |
978: | `resize_page`           | Set viewport size  | `bdg cdp Emulation.setDeviceMetricsOverride`     |
979: | `click`                 | Click element      | `bdg js "document.querySelector('...').click()"` |
980: | `type_text`             | Type into element  | `bdg js "..."`                                   |
981: | `get_page_content`      | Get page HTML      | `bdg dom query "html"`                           |
982: 
983: **Tool naming convention:** `{instance}.{instance}_{tool_name}`
984: 
985: ```typescript
986: // Example invocations
987: chrome_devtools_1.chrome_devtools_1_navigate_page({ url: "..." })
988: chrome_devtools_1.chrome_devtools_1_take_screenshot({})
989: chrome_devtools_2.chrome_devtools_2_navigate_page({ url: "..." })  // parallel instance
990: ```
991: 
992: ---
993: 
994: ## Quick Reference Card
995: 
996: ### Installation
997: 
998: ```bash

1000: ```
1001: 
1002: ### Discovery

display.text:
# Chrome DevTools Installation Guide

Complete installation and configuration guide for Chrome DevTools browser debugging, providing AI assistants with browser control through two approaches. The CLI tool (bdg) offers direct, fast, token-efficient browser debugging. The MCP fallback runs through Code Mode for multi-tool orchestration and parallel browser testing. Covers screenshot capture, DOM inspection, console evaluation, network monitoring and performance profiling.

> **Part of OpenCode Installation.** See the [Master Installation Guide](../README.md) for complete setup.
> **Package:** `browser-debugger-cli@alpha` | **Dependencies:** Node.js 18+, Chrome/Chromium

**Version:** 2.1.0 | **Updated:** 2026-02-20 | **Protocol:** Chrome DevTools Protocol (CDP)

---

## 0. AI-FIRST INSTALL GUIDE

Copy and paste this prompt to your AI assistant to get installation help:

```
I want to install the Chrome DevTools CLI (bdg) for browser debugging.

Please help me:
1. Check if I have Node.js 18+ installed
2. Install browser-debugger-cli globally
3. Verify Chrome/Chromium is available
4. Test with a basic screenshot capture
5. (Optional) Configure MCP fallback if needed

My platform is: [macOS / Linux / Windows with WSL]

Guide me through each step with the exact commands needed.
```

The AI will:
- Verify Node.js 18+ is available on your system
- Install `browser-debugger-cli` globally
- Test Chrome/Chromium availability
- Run a basic screenshot test to confirm installation
- Configure MCP fallback if you need multi-tool workflows

**Expected setup time:** 3 to 5 minutes

### Quick Success Check (30 seconds)

After installation, run these two tests immediately:

1. Open your terminal and run: `bdg --version`
2. Version output appears: CLI is working
3. In Claude Code, ask: "Take a screenshot of example.com"
4. Screenshot captured: full system is working

Not working? Go to [Troubleshooting](#9-troubleshooting).

---

## 1. OVERVIEW

Chrome DevTools gives AI assistants browser debugging capabilities through two approaches. The CLI (`bdg`) is the primary method: direct, fast and token-efficient. The MCP fallback runs through Code Mode and suits multi-tool orchestration or parallel browser testing.

### Core Principle

> **Install once, verify at each step.** Each phase has a validation checkpoint. Do not proceed until the checkpoint passes.

### Source Repository

| Property         | Value                                                                                       |
| ---------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| **GitHub (CLI)** | [szymdzum/browser-debugger-cli](https://github.com/szymdzum/browser-debugger-cli)           |
| **GitHub (MCP)** | [ChromeDevTools/chrome-devtools-mcp](https://github.com/ChromeDevTools/chrome-devtools-mcp) |
| **npm (CLI)**    | `browser-debugger-cli@alpha`                                                                |
| **npm (MCP)**    | `chrome-devtools-mcp@latest`                                                                |
| **License**      | MIT / Apache-2.0                                                                            |

### Two Approaches

| Approach            | Package                      | When to Use                             | Token Cost |
| ------------------- | ---------------------------- | --------------------------------------- | ---------- |
| **CLI (bdg)**       | `browser-debugger-cli@alpha` | Single browser, quick tasks, debugging  | Lowest     |
| **MCP (Code Mode)** | `chrome-devtools-mcp@latest` | Multi-tool workflows, parallel browsers | Higher     |

Default to CLI. Use MCP only when CLI is not sufficient.

### Decision Flowchart

```
Task received → Is bdg CLI available? (command -v bdg)
                      │
              ┌───────┴───────┐
             YES              NO
              │               │
              ▼               ▼
        Use CLI          Is Code Mode configured?
        (fastest)               │
                        ┌───────┴───────┐
                       YES              NO
                        │               │
                        ▼               ▼
                   Use MCP         Install CLI:
                   (fallback)      npm i -g browser-debugger-cli@alpha
```

### Architecture Diagram

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                    AI Client (Claude/OpenCode)               │
└─────────────────────────────┬───────────────────────────────┘
                              │
              ┌───────────────┴───────────────┐
              │                               │
              ▼                               ▼
┌─────────────────────────┐     ┌─────────────────────────────┐
│   CLI Approach (Primary)│     │   MCP Approach (Fallback)   │
│   browser-debugger-cli  │     │   Chrome DevTools MCP       │
│                         │     │   via Code Mode             │
│   - Direct terminal     │     │   - call_tool_chain()       │
│   - Self-documenting    │     │   - Type-safe invocation    │
│   - Unix composable     │     │   - Multi-tool integration  │
│   - All 644 CDP methods │     │   - 26 exposed tools        │
└────────────┬────────────┘     └──────────────┬──────────────┘
             │                                  │
             └──────────────┬───────────────────┘
                            │
                            ▼
              ┌─────────────────────────────┐
              │    Chrome DevTools Protocol │
              │    (CDP via WebSocket)      │
              │    Port 9222 (localhost)    │
              └─────────────┬───────────────┘
                            │
                            ▼
              ┌─────────────────────────────┐
              │    Chrome/Chromium/Edge     │
              └─────────────────────────────┘
```

### MCP Architecture Detail

When using MCP, Chrome DevTools routes through Code Mode, not directly to your AI client:

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  Your AI Client (Claude Code, OpenCode, VS Code)                │
│  - CLI: bdg command (direct, standalone, no Code Mode)          │
│  - MCP: 4 Code Mode tools → chrome_devtools_1.*                 │
└──────────────────────────┬──────────────────────────────────────┘
                           │ (MCP only)
                           ▼
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  Code Mode MCP (configured in opencode.json)                    │
│  - Reads .utcp_config.json for Chrome DevTools provider         │
└──────────────────────────┬──────────────────────────────────────┘
                           ▼
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  Chrome DevTools Provider (configured in .utcp_config.json)     │
│  - 26 tools via chrome_devtools_1.chrome_devtools_1_{tool}      │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

Chrome DevTools' 26 MCP tools would consume roughly 78k tokens if exposed natively. Code Mode provides on-demand access with 98% context reduction.

### Performance Targets

| Operation             | Target | Typical |
| --------------------- | ------ | ------- |
| Session startup       | <5s    | ~3s     |
| Screenshot capture    | <2s    | ~1s     |
| Console log retrieval | <1s    | ~500ms  |
| DOM query             | <500ms | ~200ms  |
| HAR export            | <3s    | ~2s     |

---

## 2. PREREQUISITES

### Required Tools

- **Node.js 18 or higher**
  ```bash
  node --version
  # Should show v18.x or higher
  ```

- **npm** (comes with Node.js)
  ```bash
  npm --version
  ```

- **Chrome/Chromium Browser**, one of:
  - Google Chrome (recommended)
  - Chromium
  - Microsoft Edge (Chromium-based)

### Platform Support

| Platform    | Status         | Notes                             |
| ----------- | -------------- | --------------------------------- |
| **macOS**   | Native support | Recommended                       |
| **Linux**   | Native support | May need sandbox configuration    |
| **Windows** | WSL only       | PowerShell and Git Bash not supported |

### Common Setup Issues

**Chrome path not found:**
```bash
# macOS
export CHROME_PATH="/Applications/Google Chrome.app/Contents/MacOS/Google Chrome"

# Linux
export CHROME_PATH="/usr/bin/google-chrome"
# or
export CHROME_PATH="/usr/bin/chromium-browser"
```

**Windows users:** WSL is required. Install it first with: `wsl --install`

---

## 3. INSTALLATION

### Step 1: Install browser-debugger-cli

```bash
npm install -g browser-debugger-cli@alpha
```

### Step 2: Verify the binary

```bash
# Confirm installation
command -v bdg || echo "Installation failed"

# Check version
bdg --version

# List available CDP domains
bdg --list
```

### Step 3: Test basic operation

```bash
# Start a session
bdg https://example.com 2>&1

# Take a screenshot
bdg screenshot test.png 2>&1

# Check console logs
bdg console logs 2>&1

# Stop the session
bdg stop 2>&1

## 4. CONFIGURATION

MCP is optional. Configure it only if you need multi-tool workflows or when CLI is not available.

### MCP Approach: Code Mode Provider

When using MCP, Chrome DevTools is a Code Mode provider, not a standalone MCP server.

| Aspect             | What This Means                                                                |
| ------------------ | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------ |
| **Configuration**  | Chrome DevTools goes in `.utcp_config.json`, not `opencode.json`               |
| **Access Method**  | Tools are accessed via Code Mode's `call_tool_chain()`                         |
| **Prerequisite**   | Code Mode MCP must be installed first. See [Code Mode INSTALL_GUIDE.md](../mcp-code-mode/INSTALL_GUIDE.md) section 4 |
| **Context Cost**   | Your AI sees only 4 Code Mode tools (~1.6k tokens), not 26 Chrome DevTools tools |
| **Naming Pattern** | `chrome_devtools_1.chrome_devtools_1_{tool_name}`                              |

### When to Use MCP

Use MCP when you:
- Already use Code Mode for other tools such as external services or Figma
- Need to chain browser operations with other MCP tools
- Require parallel browser testing to compare multiple sites at once
- Need complex multi-step automation in TypeScript
- Require type-safe tool invocation

### Step 1: Verify Code Mode

Confirm Code Mode is configured in your MCP settings. Use `.mcp.json` for Claude Code or `opencode.json` for OpenCode:

```json
{
  "mcpServers": {
    "code_mode": {
      "command": "node",
      "args": ["/path/to/code-mode-mcp/dist/index.js"],
      "env": {
        "UTCP_CONFIG_FILE": "/path/to/project/.utcp_config.json"
      }
    }
  }
}
```

### Step 2: Add Chrome DevTools to UTCP Config

Add the following to `.utcp_config.json`. Use `--isolated=true` to give each instance its own independent browser:

```json
{
  "manual_call_templates": [
    {
      "name": "chrome_devtools_1",
      "call_template_type": "mcp",
      "config": {
        "mcpServers": {
          "chrome_devtools_1": {
            "transport": "stdio",
            "command": "npx",
            "args": ["chrome-devtools-mcp@latest", "--isolated=true"],
            "env": {}
          }
        }
      }
    },
    {
      "name": "chrome_devtools_2",
      "call_template_type": "mcp",
      "config": {
        "mcpServers": {
          "chrome_devtools_2": {
            "transport": "stdio",
            "command": "npx",
            "args": ["chrome-devtools-mcp@latest", "--isolated=true"],
            "env": {}
          }
        }
      }
    }
  ]
}
```

Register multiple instances for parallel testing. Each needs `--isolated=true`.

### Step 3: Verify MCP tools

```typescript
// In Code Mode context
const tools = await list_tools();
console.log(tools.filter(t => t.includes('chrome_devtools')));
// Expected: chrome_devtools_1_navigate_page, chrome_devtools_1_take_screenshot, etc.
```

### Validation: `phase_3_complete`

| Check            | Method                  | Expected                |
| ---------------- | ----------------------- | ----------------------- |
| Config exists    | `cat .utcp_config.json` | chrome_devtools entry   |
| Tools registered | `list_tools()`          | chrome_devtools_* tools |
| Server starts    | Navigate to a URL       | No connection errors    |

❌ **STOP if validation fails.** Review your UTCP config and restart your AI client session.

### Security Considerations

Port 9222 runs the Chrome DevTools Protocol for remote debugging. It grants full browser control.

**Risks:**
- Full browser control to anyone with port access
- Cookie and session theft
- JavaScript injection
- Local file system access via browser

**Mitigations:**

| Mitigation             | Implementation                             |
| ---------------------- | ------------------------------------------ |
| Bind to localhost only | `--remote-debugging-address=127.0.0.1`     |
| Firewall rules         | Block port 9222 from external access       |
| Development only       | Never expose on production networks        |
| Network isolation      | Use in CI/CD with isolated networking      |

```bash
# Verify the port is localhost-only
lsof -i :9222
# Should show 127.0.0.1:9222, not *:9222
```

**Linux sandbox security:**

Chrome's sandbox provides process isolation. Avoid disabling it unless necessary.

| Option                     | Security Level | When to Use               |
| -------------------------- | -------------- | ------------------------- |
| Default sandbox            | Best           | Standard installations    |
| `--disable-setuid-sandbox` | Medium         | When user namespaces work |
| `--no-sandbox`             | Poor           | Last resort only          |

**Preferred approach, from most to least secure:**

1. Run in a container with proper permissions:
   ```bash
   docker run --cap-add=SYS_ADMIN chromium
   ```

2. Enable user namespaces:
   ```bash
   sudo sysctl -w kernel.unprivileged_userns_clone=1
   ```

3. Disable setuid sandbox only:
   ```bash
   export CHROME_FLAGS="--disable-setuid-sandbox"
   ```

4. No-sandbox as a last resort:
   ```bash
   export CHROME_FLAGS="--no-sandbox"
   ```

**CI/CD security flags:**
```bash
export CHROME_FLAGS="--disable-gpu --disable-dev-shm-usage --remote-debugging-address=127.0.0.1"
```

| Flag                                   | Purpose                        |
| -------------------------------------- | ------------------------------ |
| `--disable-gpu`                        | Prevent GPU issues in headless |
| `--disable-dev-shm-usage`              | Avoid shared memory issues     |
| `--remote-debugging-address=127.0.0.1` | Localhost binding only         |

## 6. USAGE

### Pattern 1: Quick Screenshot (CLI)

```bash
bdg https://example.com 2>&1
bdg screenshot output.png 2>&1
bdg stop 2>&1
```

### Pattern 2: Console Log Analysis (CLI)

```bash
bdg https://example.com 2>&1
bdg console logs 2>&1 | jq '.[] | select(.level == "error")'
bdg stop 2>&1
```

### Pattern 3: Network Monitoring (CLI)

```bash
bdg https://example.com 2>&1
bdg cdp Network.enable 2>&1
sleep 5
bdg har export network-trace.har 2>&1
bdg stop 2>&1

# Analyze slow requests
jq '.log.entries[] | select(.time > 1000) | {url: .request.url, time}' network-trace.har
```

### Pattern 4: Cookie Manipulation (CLI)

```bash
bdg https://example.com 2>&1
bdg cdp Network.enable 2>&1

# Get cookies
bdg network cookies 2>&1

# Set a cookie
bdg cdp Network.setCookie '{
  "name": "auth_token",
  "value": "secret-123",
  "domain": "example.com",
  "secure": true
}' 2>&1

bdg stop 2>&1
```

### Pattern 5: Single Instance (MCP Fallback)

When CLI is not sufficient, use Code Mode:

```typescript
call_tool_chain(`
  await chrome_devtools_1.chrome_devtools_1_navigate_page({ url: "https://example.com" });
  const screenshot = await chrome_devtools_1.chrome_devtools_1_take_screenshot({});
  const logs = await chrome_devtools_1.chrome_devtools_1_list_console_messages({});
  return { screenshot, logs };
`)
```

### Pattern 6: Parallel Instances (MCP)

Compare multiple sites at the same time:

```typescript
call_tool_chain(`
  // Instance 1: Production
  await chrome_devtools_1.chrome_devtools_1_navigate_page({
    url: "https://production.example.com"
  });

  // Instance 2: Staging (isolated browser)
  await chrome_devtools_2.chrome_devtools_2_navigate_page({
    url: "https://staging.example.com"
  });

  // Capture both screenshots
  const prod = await chrome_devtools_1.chrome_devtools_1_take_screenshot({});
  const staging = await chrome_devtools_2.chrome_devtools_2_take_screenshot({});

  return { production: prod, staging: staging };
`)
```

### Tool Selection Guide

| Scenario                 | Tool | Why                         |
| ------------------------ | ---- | --------------------------- |
| Quick screenshot         | CLI  | Fastest, lowest tokens      |
| Console log check        | CLI  | Pipe to jq for filtering    |
| Network trace            | CLI  | HAR export built in         |
| Cookie manipulation      | CLI  | Full CDP access             |
| Multi-tool workflow      | MCP  | Chain with other tools      |
| Parallel browser testing | MCP  | Multiple isolated instances |
| CDP method discovery     | CLI  | `--list`, `--describe`      |

### bdg Command Reference

**Session Commands**

| Command      | Description                     | Example                        |
| ------------ | ------------------------------- | ------------------------------ |
| `bdg <url>`  | Open URL, start browser session | `bdg https://example.com 2>&1` |
| `bdg status` | Check session status            | `bdg status 2>&1`              |
| `bdg stop`   | Stop browser, clean up session  | `bdg stop 2>&1`                |

**Helper Commands**

| Command                      | Description             | Example                             |
| ---------------------------- | ----------------------- | ----------------------------------- |
| `bdg screenshot <path>`      | Capture page screenshot | `bdg screenshot /tmp/test.png 2>&1` |
| `bdg console logs`           | Get console messages    | `bdg console logs 2>&1 \| jq '.'`   |
| `bdg network cookies`        | Get all cookies         | `bdg network cookies 2>&1`          |
| `bdg dom query "<selector>"` | Query DOM elements      | `bdg dom query ".my-class" 2>&1`    |
| `bdg js "<expression>"`      | Execute JavaScript      | `bdg js "document.title" 2>&1`      |
| `bdg har export <path>`      | Export network as HAR   | `bdg har export trace.har 2>&1`     |

**Discovery Commands**

| Command                   | Description               | Example                                 |
| ------------------------- | ------------------------- | --------------------------------------- |
| `bdg --version`           | Show CLI version          | `bdg --version`                         |
| `bdg --list`              | List all CDP domains (53) | `bdg --list`                            |
| `bdg --describe <domain>` | Show domain methods       | `bdg --describe Page`                   |
| `bdg --describe <method>` | Show method signature     | `bdg --describe Page.captureScreenshot` |
| `bdg --search <term>`     | Search CDP methods        | `bdg --search screenshot`               |

**Raw CDP Commands**

| Command                     | Description         | Example                                                     |
| --------------------------- | ------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------- |
| `bdg cdp <method>`          | Execute CDP method  | `bdg cdp Page.reload 2>&1`                                  |
| `bdg cdp <method> '<json>'` | CDP with parameters | `bdg cdp Network.setCookie '{"name":"x","value":"y"}' 2>&1` |

**Error Handling Pattern**

Always use `2>&1` to capture stderr for error handling:

```bash
#!/bin/bash
trap "bdg stop 2>&1" EXIT INT TERM
command -v bdg || { echo "Install bdg first"; exit 1; }
bdg "$URL" 2>&1 || exit 1
# ... operations ...
# Cleanup runs automatically on exit
```

## 8. EXAMPLES

### Example 1: Full Debug Session

```bash
#!/bin/bash
# Complete debug session with error handling
trap "bdg stop 2>&1" EXIT INT TERM

# Verify CLI is available
command -v bdg || { echo "Install: npm install -g browser-debugger-cli@alpha"; exit 1; }

# Start session
bdg https://example.com 2>&1 || exit 1

# Capture initial state
bdg screenshot before.png 2>&1
bdg console logs 2>&1 > console-before.json

# Enable network monitoring
bdg cdp Network.enable 2>&1
sleep 3

# Export network trace
bdg har export session.har 2>&1
bdg screenshot after.png 2>&1

echo "Debug session complete. Files: before.png, after.png, session.har, console-before.json"
```

### Example 2: JavaScript Execution

```bash
bdg https://example.com 2>&1

# Get page title
bdg js "document.title" 2>&1

# Count links on page
bdg js "document.querySelectorAll('a').length" 2>&1

# Get all link hrefs
bdg js "Array.from(document.querySelectorAll('a')).map(a => a.href)" 2>&1

bdg stop 2>&1
```

### Example 3: DOM Query and Interaction

```bash
bdg https://example.com 2>&1

# Query elements by class
bdg dom query ".nav-link" 2>&1

# Click a button via JavaScript
bdg js "document.querySelector('#submit-btn').click()" 2>&1

# Check result after click
bdg screenshot post-click.png 2>&1
bdg console logs 2>&1

bdg stop 2>&1
```

### Example 4: Network Analysis

```bash
bdg https://example.com 2>&1
bdg cdp Network.enable 2>&1

# Wait for full page load activity
sleep 5
bdg har export network-trace.har 2>&1
bdg stop 2>&1

# Find slow requests (over 1 second)
jq '.log.entries[] | select(.time > 1000) | {url: .request.url, time}' network-trace.har

# Find failed requests
jq '.log.entries[] | select(.response.status >= 400) | {url: .request.url, status: .response.status}' network-trace.har
```

### Example 5: Parallel Comparison (MCP)

```typescript
// Compare production and staging side by side
call_tool_chain(`
  await chrome_devtools_1.chrome_devtools_1_navigate_page({
    url: "https://production.example.com"
  });
  await chrome_devtools_2.chrome_devtools_2_navigate_page({
    url: "https://staging.example.com"
  });

  const prodScreenshot = await chrome_devtools_1.chrome_devtools_1_take_screenshot({});
  const stagingScreenshot = await chrome_devtools_2.chrome_devtools_2_take_screenshot({});
  const prodLogs = await chrome_devtools_1.chrome_devtools_1_list_console_messages({});
  const stagingLogs = await chrome_devtools_2.chrome_devtools_2_list_console_messages({});

  return {
    production: { screenshot: prodScreenshot, logs: prodLogs },
    staging: { screenshot: stagingScreenshot, logs: stagingLogs }
  };
`)
```

### Example 6: Cookie Session Management

```bash
bdg https://example.com 2>&1
bdg cdp Network.enable 2>&1

# Export current cookies
bdg network cookies 2>&1 > cookies.json

# Inject an auth token
bdg cdp Network.setCookie '{
  "name": "auth_token",
  "value": "test-session-token",
  "domain": "example.com",
  "path": "/",
  "secure": true,
  "httpOnly": true
}' 2>&1

# Reload to test authenticated state
bdg cdp Page.reload 2>&1
bdg screenshot authenticated.png 2>&1

bdg stop 2>&1
```

## 9. TROUBLESHOOTING

### Error/Cause/Fix Reference

| Error | Cause | Fix |
| ----- | ----- | --- |
| `command not found: bdg` | CLI not installed or not in PATH | Run `npm install -g browser-debugger-cli@alpha`. Check PATH with `npm config get prefix` |
| `Error: Could not find Chrome` | Chrome path not set or binary missing | Set `CHROME_PATH` env var. See path examples below |
| `Error: No active session` | Session not started or crashed | Run `bdg stop 2>&1` then retry `bdg <url> 2>&1` |
| `Failed to move to new namespace` | Linux sandbox restriction | Enable user namespaces or set `CHROME_FLAGS`. See Linux sandbox section |
| `bdg fails on Windows` | Native Windows not supported | Install WSL with `wsl --install`, then install bdg inside WSL |
| Chrome DevTools tools missing in Code Mode | UTCP config missing or disabled | Check `.utcp_config.json`, confirm `disabled: false`, restart your session |
| MCP server fails to start | Port conflict or config error | Run `lsof -i :9222` and kill conflicting processes |
| `EADDRINUSE: address already in use :::9222` | Port 9222 already taken | Run `kill -9 $(lsof -t -i :9222)` to free the port |

### CLI: Command Not Found

```bash
# Install bdg
npm install -g browser-debugger-cli@alpha

# Or fix PATH
npm config get prefix
export PATH="$(npm config get prefix)/bin:$PATH"

# Verify
command -v bdg
```

### CLI: Chrome Not Found

```bash
# macOS
export CHROME_PATH="/Applications/Google Chrome.app/Contents/MacOS/Google Chrome"

# Linux
export CHROME_PATH="/usr/bin/google-chrome"

# Confirm Chrome exists at the path
ls -la "$CHROME_PATH"

# Add to your shell profile
echo 'export CHROME_PATH="/path/to/chrome"' >> ~/.zshrc
```

### CLI: Session Won't Start

```bash
# Force stop any existing sessions
bdg stop 2>&1

# Find zombie Chrome processes
ps aux | grep -i chrome | grep -i debug

# Kill zombie processes
pkill -f "chrome.*remote-debugging"

# Retry
bdg https://example.com 2>&1
```

### CLI: Sandbox Errors (Linux)

Fix in order of preference:

```bash
# Option 1: Enable user namespaces (preferred)
sudo sysctl -w kernel.unprivileged_userns_clone=1

# Option 2: Disable setuid sandbox only
export CHROME_FLAGS="--disable-setuid-sandbox"

# Option 3: No sandbox (least secure, last resort)
export CHROME_FLAGS="--no-sandbox"
```

### CLI: Windows Not Supported

```bash
# Install WSL
wsl --install

# Inside WSL, install bdg
npm install -g browser-debugger-cli@alpha
```

### MCP: Tool Not Found

1. Open `.utcp_config.json` and confirm the chrome_devtools entry exists
2. Check that `disabled` is not set to `true`
3. Restart your Claude Code or OpenCode session
4. Run `list_tools()` and look for chrome_devtools_* entries

### MCP: Connection Failed

```bash
# Test the MCP server directly
npx chrome-devtools-mcp@latest --version

# Check for port conflicts
lsof -i :9222

# Kill any conflicting processes
kill $(lsof -t -i :9222)
```

### MCP: Port Already in Use

```bash
# Find the process using port 9222
lsof -i :9222

# Kill it
kill -9 $(lsof -t -i :9222)

# Confirm the port is free
lsof -i :9222
# Should return nothing
```

14: Progressive disclosure from discovery to execution - always explore capabilities via `bdg cdp --list`/`--describe`/`--search` before executing CDP methods. Self-documentation eliminates hardcoded method lists and ensures up-to-date access to 300+ CDP methods across 53 domains.
15: 
16: ---
17: 
18: ## 2. PREREQUISITES
19: 
20: **Foundation:** Follow session management basics from SKILL.md Section 3 for all CDP operations:
21: - **Installation Verification**: Check `command -v bdg` before first use
22: - **Session Lifecycle**: Start → Verify → Execute → Stop pattern
23: - **Error Handling**: Always use `2>&1` for stderr capture
24: - See [../SKILL.md](../SKILL.md) Section 3 for core workflows
25: 
26: **Platform Requirements**:
27: - macOS: Native support
28: - Linux: Native support
29: - Windows: WSL only (PowerShell/Git Bash NOT supported)
30: 
31: ---
32: 
33: ## 3. QUICK REFERENCE
34: 
35: **Common Use Cases**:
36: 
37: | Task | Command Pattern | Output |
38: |------|----------------|--------|
39: | Screenshot | `bdg dom screenshot output.png` | PNG file saved |
40: | Console logs | `bdg console --list` | JSON array of log entries |
41: | Network cookies | `bdg network getCookies` | JSON cookie list |
42: | DOM query | `bdg dom query ".classname"` | Matching elements |
43: | HAR export | `bdg network har output.har` | HAR file with network data |
44: | JavaScript eval | `bdg dom eval "document.title"` | Execution result |
45: | Page navigation | `bdg cdp Page.navigate '{"url":"https://example.com"}'` | Navigation response |
46: 
47: ---
48: 
49: ## 4. CDP DOMAIN PATTERNS
50: 
51: ### Page Domain
52: 
53: **Navigation and Lifecycle**:
54: ```bash
55: # Navigate to URL
56: bdg cdp Page.navigate '{"url":"https://example.com"}'
57: 
58: # Get navigation history
59: bdg cdp Page.getNavigationHistory
60: 
61: # Reload page
62: bdg cdp Page.reload '{"ignoreCache":true}'
63: 
64: # Stop loading
65: bdg cdp Page.stopLoading
66: 
67: # Enable page events
68: bdg cdp Page.enable
69: ```
70: 
71: **Screenshot and Rendering**:
72: ```bash
73: # Full page screenshot (base64)
74: bdg cdp Page.captureScreenshot
75: 
76: # Specific viewport screenshot
77: bdg cdp Page.captureScreenshot '{
78:   "clip": {
79:     "x": 0,
80:     "y": 0,
81:     "width": 800,
82:     "height": 600,
83:     "scale": 1
84:   }
85: }'
86: 
87: # Print to PDF
88: bdg cdp Page.printToPDF '{"landscape":false}'
89: ```
90: 
91: **Layout and Metrics**:
92: ```bash
93: # Get layout metrics
94: bdg cdp Page.getLayoutMetrics
95: 
96: # Set device metrics override
97: bdg cdp Emulation.setDeviceMetricsOverride '{
98:   "width": 375,
99:   "height": 667,
100:   "deviceScaleFactor": 2,
101:   "mobile": true
102: }'
103: ```
104: 
105: ---
106: 
107: ### DOM Domain
108: 
109: **Document Inspection**:
110: ```bash
111: # Get document root
112: bdg cdp DOM.getDocument
113: 
114: # Get document with depth
115: bdg cdp DOM.getDocument '{"depth":-1,"pierce":true}'
116: 
117: # Query selector
118: bdg cdp DOM.querySelector '{
119:   "nodeId": 1,
120:   "selector": ".my-class"
121: }'
122: 
123: # Query all matching
124: bdg cdp DOM.querySelectorAll '{
125:   "nodeId": 1,
126:   "selector": "div"
127: }'
128: ```
129: 
130: **Node Operations**:
131: ```bash
132: # Get outer HTML
133: bdg cdp DOM.getOuterHTML '{"nodeId":123}'
134: 
135: # Get attributes
136: bdg cdp DOM.getAttributes '{"nodeId":123}'
137: 
138: # Set attribute value
139: bdg cdp DOM.setAttributeValue '{
140:   "nodeId": 123,
141:   "name": "class",
142:   "value": "active"
143: }'
144: 
145: # Remove node
146: bdg cdp DOM.removeNode '{"nodeId":123}'
147: ```
148: 
149: **Helper Command Alternative**:
150: ```bash
151: # Simpler DOM query using helper
152: bdg dom query "button.submit"
153: bdg dom query "#main-content"
154: bdg dom query "[data-testid='login']"
155: ```
156: 
157: ---
158: 
159: ### Network Domain
160: 
161: **Network Monitoring**:
162: ```bash
163: # Enable network tracking
164: bdg cdp Network.enable
165: 
166: # Clear browser cache
167: bdg cdp Network.clearBrowserCache
168: 
169: # Clear browser cookies
170: bdg cdp Network.clearBrowserCookies
171: 
172: # Get all cookies
173: bdg cdp Network.getAllCookies
174: 
175: # Get cookies for URL
176: bdg cdp Network.getCookies '{"urls":["https://example.com"]}'
177: 
178: # Set cookie
179: bdg cdp Network.setCookie '{
180:   "name": "session",
181:   "value": "abc123",
182:   "domain": "example.com",
183:   "path": "/",
184:   "secure": true,
185:   "httpOnly": true
186: }'
187: 
188: # Delete cookie
189: bdg cdp Network.deleteCookies '{
190:   "name": "session",
191:   "domain": "example.com"
192: }'
193: ```
194: 
195: **Request Interception**:
196: ```bash
197: # Enable request interception
198: bdg cdp Network.setRequestInterception '{
199:   "patterns": [{"urlPattern":"*"}]
200: }'
201: 
202: # Continue intercepted request
203: bdg cdp Network.continueInterceptedRequest '{
204:   "interceptionId": "id-123"
205: }'
206: 
207: # Block request
208: bdg cdp Network.continueInterceptedRequest '{
209:   "interceptionId": "id-123",
210:   "errorReason": "BlockedByClient"
211: }'
212: ```
213: 
214: **Helper Command Alternative**:
215: ```bash
216: # Simpler network inspection
217: bdg network getCookies
218: bdg network headers
219: bdg network har network-trace.har
220: ```
221: 
222: ---
223: 
224: ### Runtime Domain
225: 
226: **Console and Logging**:
227: ```bash
228: # Enable console
229: bdg cdp Runtime.enable
230: 
231: # Evaluate JavaScript
232: bdg cdp Runtime.evaluate '{
233:   "expression": "document.title",
234:   "returnByValue": true
235: }'
236: 
237: # Call function on object
238: bdg cdp Runtime.callFunctionOn '{
239:   "functionDeclaration": "function() { return this.value; }",
240:   "objectId": "object-123"
241: }'
242: 
243: # Get properties
244: bdg cdp Runtime.getProperties '{
245:   "objectId": "object-123",
246:   "ownProperties": true
247: }'
248: ```
249: 
250: **Helper Command Alternative**:
251: ```bash
252: # Simpler console access
253: bdg console --list
254: bdg dom eval "window.location.href"
255: bdg dom eval "localStorage.getItem('token')"
256: ```
257: 
258: ---
259: 
260: ### Memory Domain
261: 
262: **Heap Snapshots**:
263: ```bash
264: # Take heap snapshot
265: bdg cdp HeapProfiler.takeHeapSnapshot
266: 
267: # Start sampling
268: bdg cdp HeapProfiler.startSampling
269: 
270: # Stop sampling
271: bdg cdp HeapProfiler.stopSampling
272: 
273: # Get sampling profile
274: bdg cdp HeapProfiler.getSamplingProfile
275: ```
276: 
277: **Memory Metrics**:
278: ```bash
279: # Get DOM counters
280: bdg cdp Memory.getDOMCounters
281: 
282: # Force garbage collection
283: bdg cdp HeapProfiler.collectGarbage
284: ```
285: 
286: ---
287: 
288: ### Performance Domain
289: 
290: **Metrics Collection**:
291: ```bash
292: # Enable performance tracking
293: bdg cdp Performance.enable
294: 
295: # Get metrics
296: bdg cdp Performance.getMetrics
297: 
298: # Disable tracking
299: bdg cdp Performance.disable
300: ```
301: 
302: **Timeline Recording**:
303: ```bash
304: # Start timeline
305: bdg cdp Tracing.start '{
306:   "categories": "devtools.timeline"
307: }'
308: 
309: # Stop timeline
310: bdg cdp Tracing.end
311: 
312: # Get trace
313: bdg cdp Tracing.getCategories
314: ```
315: 
316: ---
317: 
318: ## 5. COMPLETE WORKFLOWS
319: 
320: ### Workflow 1: Full Page Screenshot
321: 
322: ```bash
323: #!/bin/bash
324: # Complete screenshot workflow with error handling
325: 
326: # Check bdg availability
327: command -v bdg || { echo "Install: npm install -g browser-debugger-cli@alpha"; exit 1; }
328: 
329: # Start session
330: bdg https://example.com 2>&1
331: 
332: # Wait for page load (check status)
333: sleep 2
334: bdg status 2>&1 || { echo "Session failed"; exit 1; }
335: 
336: # Capture screenshot
337: bdg dom screenshot output.png 2>&1
338: 
339: # Alternative: Base64 screenshot via CDP
340: bdg cdp Page.captureScreenshot 2>&1 | jq -r '.result.data' > screenshot-b64.txt
341: 
342: # Stop session
343: bdg stop 2>&1
344: 
345: echo "Screenshot saved to output.png"
346: ```
347: 
348: ---
349: 
350: ### Workflow 2: Console Log Analysis
351: 
352: ```bash
353: #!/bin/bash
354: # Capture and analyze console logs
355: 
356: bdg https://example.com 2>&1
357: 
358: # Enable console
359: bdg cdp Runtime.enable 2>&1
360: 
361: # Get console logs (helper)
362: bdg console --list 2>&1 | jq '.[] | select(.level=="error")' > errors.json
363: 
364: # Count errors
365: error_count=$(jq '. | length' errors.json)
366: echo "Found $error_count console errors"
367: 
368: # Extract error messages
369: jq -r '.[] | "\(.level): \(.text)"' errors.json
370: 
371: bdg stop 2>&1
372: ```
373: 
374: ---
375: 
376: ### Workflow 3: Network Request Monitoring
377: 
378: ```bash
379: #!/bin/bash
380: # Monitor and export network requests
381: 
382: bdg https://example.com 2>&1
383: 
384: # Enable network tracking
385: bdg cdp Network.enable 2>&1
386: 
387: # Wait for page load
388: sleep 5
389: 
390: # Export HAR file
391: bdg network har network-trace.har 2>&1
392: 
393: # Analyze HAR with jq
394: jq '.log.entries[] | {
395:   url: .request.url,
396:   method: .request.method,
397:   status: .response.status,
398:   time: .time
399: }' network-trace.har > requests-summary.json
400: 
401: # Find slow requests (>1000ms)
402: jq '.log.entries[] | select(.time > 1000) | {url, time}' network-trace.har
403: 
404: bdg stop 2>&1
405: ```
406: 
407: ---
408: 
409: ### Workflow 4: Cookie Manipulation
410: 
411: ```bash
412: #!/bin/bash
413: # Cookie operations workflow
414: 
415: bdg https://example.com 2>&1
416: 
417: # Enable network
418: bdg cdp Network.enable 2>&1
419: 
420: # Get all cookies
421: bdg network getCookies 2>&1 | jq '.'
422: 
423: # Set authentication cookie
424: bdg cdp Network.setCookie '{
425:   "name": "auth_token",
426:   "value": "secret-token-123",
427:   "domain": "example.com",
428:   "path": "/",
429:   "secure": true,
430:   "httpOnly": true,
431:   "sameSite": "Strict"
432: }' 2>&1
433: 
434: # Verify cookie set
435: bdg cdp Network.getCookies '{"urls":["https://example.com"]}' 2>&1 | \
436:   jq '.result.cookies[] | select(.name=="auth_token")'
437: 
438: # Navigate with cookie
439: bdg cdp Page.navigate '{"url":"https://example.com/dashboard"}' 2>&1
440: 
441: bdg stop 2>&1
442: ```
443: 
444: ---
445: 
446: ### Workflow 5: DOM Inspection and Modification
447: 
448: ```bash
449: #!/bin/bash
450: # DOM inspection and modification
451: 
452: bdg https://example.com 2>&1
453: 
454: # Get document
455: doc_node=$(bdg cdp DOM.getDocument 2>&1 | jq '.result.root.nodeId')
456: 
457: # Find element by selector
458: element_node=$(bdg cdp DOM.querySelector "{
459:   \"nodeId\": $doc_node,
460:   \"selector\": \"h1\"
461: }" 2>&1 | jq '.result.nodeId')
462: 
463: # Get element HTML
464: bdg cdp DOM.getOuterHTML "{\"nodeId\": $element_node}" 2>&1 | jq -r '.result.outerHTML'
465: 
466: # Modify attribute
467: bdg cdp DOM.setAttributeValue "{
468:   \"nodeId\": $element_node,
469:   \"name\": \"class\",
470:   \"value\": \"modified\"
471: }" 2>&1
472: 
473: # Alternative: Use helper for simpler queries
474: bdg dom query "h1" 2>&1
475: 
476: bdg stop 2>&1
477: ```
478: 
479: ---
480: 
481: ## 6. UNIX COMPOSABILITY PATTERNS
482: 
483: ### Pattern 1: Pipe to jq for JSON Processing
484: 
485: ```bash
486: # Extract specific fields
487: bdg cdp Page.getNavigationHistory 2>&1 | jq '.result.entries[] | .url'
488: 
489: # Filter results
490: bdg console --list 2>&1 | jq '.[] | select(.level == "error")'
491: 
492: # Transform output
493: bdg network getCookies 2>&1 | jq '[.[] | {name, domain, value}]'
494: 
495: # Count items
496: bdg cdp DOM.querySelectorAll '{"nodeId":1,"selector":"div"}' 2>&1 | \
497:   jq '.result.nodeIds | length'
498: ```
499: 
500: ---
501: 
502: ### Pattern 2: Grep for Text Filtering
503: 
504: ```bash
505: # Find errors in logs
506: bdg console --list 2>&1 | grep -i "error"
507: 
508: # Filter by URL pattern
509: bdg network har - 2>&1 | grep "api.example.com"
510: 
511: # Case-insensitive search
512: bdg cdp DOM.getDocument 2>&1 | grep -i "title"
513: ```
514: 
515: ---
516: 
517: ### Pattern 3: Combine with curl and Other Tools
518: 
519: ```bash
520: # Download screenshot and upload to S3
521: bdg dom screenshot - 2>&1 | aws s3 cp - s3://bucket/screenshot.png
522: 
523: # Extract data and POST to API
524: bdg dom eval "localStorage.getItem('data')" 2>&1 | \
525:   jq -r '.result.value' | \
526:   curl -X POST -d @- https://api.example.com/data
527: 
528: # Chain multiple operations
529: bdg cdp Page.captureScreenshot 2>&1 | \
530:   jq -r '.result.data' | \
531:   base64 -d > output.png
532: ```
533: 
534: ---
535: 
536: ### Pattern 4: Loop Over Results
537: 
538: ```bash
539: # Process multiple URLs
540: urls=("https://example.com" "https://test.com" "https://demo.com")
541: 
542: for url in "${urls[@]}"; do
543:   bdg "$url" 2>&1
544:   bdg dom screenshot "${url//https:\/\//}.png" 2>&1
545:   bdg stop 2>&1
546: done
547: 
548: # Batch cookie extraction
549: bdg https://example.com 2>&1
550: bdg network getCookies 2>&1 | jq -r '.[] | "\(.name)=\(.value)"' | \
551: while read cookie; do
552:   echo "$cookie" >> cookies.txt
553: done
554: bdg stop 2>&1
555: ```
556: 
557: ---
558: 
559: ### Pattern 5: Error Stream Separation
560: 
561: ```bash
562: # Capture stderr separately
563: bdg cdp Page.navigate '{"url":"invalid"}' 2>errors.log 1>output.json
564: 
565: # Check exit code
566: bdg status 2>&1
567: if [ $? -ne 0 ]; then
568:   echo "Session check failed"
569:   cat errors.log
570: fi
571: 
572: # Discard stderr
573: bdg cdp Network.enable 2>/dev/null
574: 
575: # Combine and filter
576: (bdg console --list 2>&1 | jq '.') || echo "Command failed"
577: ```
578: 
579: ---
580: 
581: ## 7. DISCOVERY PATTERN EXAMPLES
582: 
583: ### Before/After: Progressive Disclosure
584: 
585: ❌ **BEFORE** (Anti-pattern - Guessing method names):
586: ```bash
587: # Guessing method names without discovery
588: bdg cdp Page.screenshot 2>&1  # FAILS - wrong method name
589: bdg cdp Screenshot 2>&1       # FAILS - wrong domain
590: bdg cdp capture 2>&1          # FAILS - incomplete name
591: 
592: # Results in: "Error: Method not found"
593: # Wastes time with trial and error
594: ```
595: 
596: ✅ **AFTER** (Correct - Progressive disclosure):
597: ```bash
598: # Step 1: List all domains
599: bdg cdp --list  # → Discover "Page" domain exists
600: 
601: **Validation**: `domains_discovered`
602: 
603: # Step 2: Explore specific domain
604: bdg cdp --describe Page  # → Find "captureScreenshot" method
605: 
606: **Validation**: `method_identified`
607: 
608: # Step 3: Get method details
609: bdg cdp --describe Page.captureScreenshot  # → See parameters and return type
610: 
611: **Validation**: `signature_understood`
612: 
613: # Step 4: Execute discovered method
614: bdg cdp Page.captureScreenshot 2>&1
615: 
616: **Validation**: `execution_successful`
617: ```
618: 
619: **Why better**: No guessing, documentation always current, clear understanding before execution, eliminates trial-and-error.
620: 
621: **Finding Methods by Use Case**:
622: 
623: ```bash
624: # Find cookie-related methods
625: bdg cdp --search cookie
626: 
627: # Find performance methods
628: bdg cdp --search performance
629: 
630: # Find DOM manipulation
631: bdg cdp --search DOM
632: 
633: # Find network interception
634: bdg cdp --search intercept
635: ```
636: 
637: ---
638: 
639: ## 8. ADVANCED PATTERNS
640: 
641: ### Multi-Session Management
642: 
643: ```bash
644: # Session 1: Monitor network
645: bdg https://example.com 2>&1 &
646: SESSION_1_PID=$!
647: 
648: # Session 2: Different URL
649: bdg https://test.com 2>&1 &
650: SESSION_2_PID=$!
651: 
652: # Check both sessions
653: bdg status 2>&1
654: 
655: # Stop specific session (requires manual intervention or session IDs)
656: kill $SESSION_1_PID
657: kill $SESSION_2_PID
658: ```
659: 
660: ### Conditional Execution
661: 
662: ```bash
663: # Execute CDP only if session active
664: if bdg status 2>&1 | grep -q "active"; then
665:   bdg cdp Page.captureScreenshot 2>&1
666: else
667:   echo "No active session"
668: fi
669: 
670: # Retry with exponential backoff
671: for i in {1..5}; do
672:   bdg https://example.com 2>&1 && break
673:   echo "Retry $i failed, waiting..."
674:   sleep $((2**i))
675: done
676: ```
677: 
678: ### Data Extraction Pipelines
679: 
680: ```bash
681: # Extract, transform, load pattern
682: bdg https://example.com 2>&1 && \
683: bdg dom eval "document.querySelectorAll('a')" 2>&1 | \
684: jq -r '.result.value[] | .href' | \
685: sort -u > links.txt && \
686: bdg stop 2>&1
687: 
688: # Performance monitoring pipeline
689: bdg https://example.com 2>&1 && \
690: bdg cdp Performance.enable 2>&1 && \
691: sleep 5 && \
692: bdg cdp Performance.getMetrics 2>&1 | \
693: jq '.result.metrics[] | select(.name | contains("DOM"))' > perf.json && \
694: bdg stop 2>&1
695: ```
696: 
697: ---
698: 
699: ## 9. BEST PRACTICES
700: 
701: 1. **Always verify session status** before executing CDP commands
702: 2. **Use helpers when available** (simpler than raw CDP)
703: 3. **Capture stderr** with `2>&1` for comprehensive error handling
704: 4. **Use jq for JSON processing** instead of string manipulation
705: 5. **Clean up sessions** with `bdg stop` after completion
706: 6. **Progressive disclosure** - start with --describe before executing
707: 7. **Case-insensitive method names** - bdg normalizes automatically
708: 8. **Semantic exit codes** - check `$?` for scripting
709: 
710: ---
711: 
712: ## 10. RELATED RESOURCES
713: 
714: ### Reference Files
715: - [session_management.md](./session_management.md) - Advanced session lifecycle patterns and cleanup strategies
716: - [troubleshooting.md](./troubleshooting.md) - Error resolution and diagnostic procedures
717: 
718: ### Related Skills
719: - `mcp-chrome-devtools` - Main skill orchestrator for bdg CLI workflows
720: 
721: ### External Resources
722: - [Chrome DevTools Protocol](https://chromedevtools.github.io/devtools-protocol/) - Official CDP documentation with 300+ methods across 53 domains
723: - [browser-debugger-cli GitHub](https://github.com/szymdzum/browser-debugger-cli) - Source repository and issue tracker
724: 
725: ---
726: 
727: **Reference**: Full CDP documentation at https://chromedevtools.github.io/devtools-protocol/

# CDP Patterns & Command Examples

CDP command reference with domain patterns and Unix pipeline examples.

---

## 1. OVERVIEW

Progressive disclosure from discovery to execution - always explore capabilities via `bdg cdp --list`/`--describe`/`--search` before executing CDP methods. Self-documentation eliminates hardcoded method lists and ensures up-to-date access to 300+ CDP methods across 53 domains.

---

## 2. PREREQUISITES

**Foundation:** Follow session management basics from SKILL.md Section 3 for all CDP operations:
- **Installation Verification**: Check `command -v bdg` before first use
- **Session Lifecycle**: Start → Verify → Execute → Stop pattern
- **Error Handling**: Always use `2>&1` for stderr capture
- See [../SKILL.md](../SKILL.md) Section 3 for core workflows

**Platform Requirements**:
- macOS: Native support
- Linux: Native support
- Windows: WSL only (PowerShell/Git Bash NOT supported)

---

## 3. QUICK REFERENCE

**Common Use Cases**:

| Task | Command Pattern | Output |
|------|----------------|--------|
| Screenshot | `bdg dom screenshot output.png` | PNG file saved |
| Console logs | `bdg console --list` | JSON array of log entries |
| Network cookies | `bdg network getCookies` | JSON cookie list |
| DOM query | `bdg dom query ".classname"` | Matching elements |
| HAR export | `bdg network har output.har` | HAR file with network data |
| JavaScript eval | `bdg dom eval "document.title"` | Execution result |
| Page navigation | `bdg cdp Page.navigate '{"url":"https://example.com"}'` | Navigation response |

---

## 4. CDP DOMAIN PATTERNS

### Page Domain

**Navigation and Lifecycle**:
```bash
# Navigate to URL
bdg cdp Page.navigate '{"url":"https://example.com"}'

# Get navigation history
bdg cdp Page.getNavigationHistory

# Reload page
bdg cdp Page.reload '{"ignoreCache":true}'

# Stop loading
bdg cdp Page.stopLoading

# Enable page events
bdg cdp Page.enable
```

**Screenshot and Rendering**:
```bash
# Full page screenshot (base64)
bdg cdp Page.captureScreenshot

# Specific viewport screenshot
bdg cdp Page.captureScreenshot '{
  "clip": {
    "x": 0,
    "y": 0,
    "width": 800,
    "height": 600,
    "scale": 1
  }
}'

# Print to PDF
bdg cdp Page.printToPDF '{"landscape":false}'
```

**Layout and Metrics**:
```bash
# Get layout metrics
bdg cdp Page.getLayoutMetrics

# Set device metrics override
bdg cdp Emulation.setDeviceMetricsOverride '{
  "width": 375,
  "height": 667,
  "deviceScaleFactor": 2,

## 5. COMPLETE WORKFLOWS

### Workflow 1: Full Page Screenshot

```bash
#!/bin/bash
# Complete screenshot workflow with error handling

# Check bdg availability
command -v bdg || { echo "Install: npm install -g browser-debugger-cli@alpha"; exit 1; }

# Start session
bdg https://example.com 2>&1

# Wait for page load (check status)
sleep 2
bdg status 2>&1 || { echo "Session failed"; exit 1; }

# Capture screenshot
bdg dom screenshot output.png 2>&1

# Alternative: Base64 screenshot via CDP
bdg cdp Page.captureScreenshot 2>&1 | jq -r '.result.data' > screenshot-b64.txt

# Stop session
bdg stop 2>&1

echo "Screenshot saved to output.png"
```

---

### Workflow 2: Console Log Analysis

```bash
#!/bin/bash
# Capture and analyze console logs

bdg https://example.com 2>&1

# Enable console
bdg cdp Runtime.enable 2>&1

# Get console logs (helper)
bdg console --list 2>&1 | jq '.[] | select(.level=="error")' > errors.json

# Count errors
error_count=$(jq '. | length' errors.json)
echo "Found $error_count console errors"

# Extract error messages
jq -r '.[] | "\(.level): \(.text)"' errors.json

bdg stop 2>&1
```

---

### Workflow 3: Network Request Monitoring

```bash
#!/bin/bash
# Monitor and export network requests

bdg https://example.com 2>&1

# Enable network tracking
bdg cdp Network.enable 2>&1

# Wait for page load
sleep 5

# Export HAR file
bdg network har network-trace.har 2>&1

# Analyze HAR with jq
jq '.log.entries[] | {
  url: .request.url,
  method: .request.method,
  status: .response.status,
  time: .time
}' network-trace.har > requests-summary.json

# Find slow requests (>1000ms)
jq '.log.entries[] | select(.time > 1000) | {url, time}' network-trace.har

bdg stop 2>&1
```

bdg https://example.com 2>&1

bdg https://example.com 2>&1

# Find element by selector

## 6. UNIX COMPOSABILITY PATTERNS

### Pattern 1: Pipe to jq for JSON Processing

```bash
# Extract specific fields
bdg cdp Page.getNavigationHistory 2>&1 | jq '.result.entries[] | .url'

# Filter results
bdg console --list 2>&1 | jq '.[] | select(.level == "error")'

# Transform output
bdg network getCookies 2>&1 | jq '[.[] | {name, domain, value}]'

# Count items
bdg cdp DOM.querySelectorAll '{"nodeId":1,"selector":"div"}' 2>&1 | \
  jq '.result.nodeIds | length'
```

---

### Pattern 2: Grep for Text Filtering

```bash
# Find errors in logs
bdg console --list 2>&1 | grep -i "error"

# Filter by URL pattern
bdg network har - 2>&1 | grep "api.example.com"

# Case-insensitive search
bdg cdp DOM.getDocument 2>&1 | grep -i "title"
```

---

### Pattern 3: Combine with curl and Other Tools

```bash
# Download screenshot and upload to S3
bdg dom screenshot - 2>&1 | aws s3 cp - s3://bucket/screenshot.png

# Extract data and POST to API
bdg dom eval "localStorage.getItem('data')" 2>&1 | \
  jq -r '.result.value' | \
  curl -X POST -d @- https://api.example.com/data

# Chain multiple operations
bdg cdp Page.captureScreenshot 2>&1 | \
  jq -r '.result.data' | \
  base64 -d > output.png
```

---

### Pattern 4: Loop Over Results

```bash
# Process multiple URLs
urls=("https://example.com" "https://test.com" "https://demo.com")

for url in "${urls[@]}"; do
  bdg "$url" 2>&1
  bdg dom screenshot "${url//https:\/\//}.png" 2>&1
  bdg stop 2>&1
done

# Batch cookie extraction
bdg https://example.com 2>&1
bdg network getCookies 2>&1 | jq -r '.[] | "\(.name)=\(.value)"' | \
while read cookie; do
  echo "$cookie" >> cookies.txt
done
bdg stop 2>&1
```

---

### Pattern 5: Error Stream Separation

```bash
# Capture stderr separately
bdg cdp Page.navigate '{"url":"invalid"}' 2>errors.log 1>output.json

# Check exit code
bdg status 2>&1
if [ $? -ne 0 ]; then
  echo "Session check failed"
  cat errors.log
fi

# Discard stderr
bdg cdp Network.enable 2>/dev/null

# Combine and filter
(bdg console --list 2>&1 | jq '.') || echo "Command failed"
```

---

## 7. DISCOVERY PATTERN EXAMPLES

### Before/After: Progressive Disclosure

❌ **BEFORE** (Anti-pattern - Guessing method names):
```bash
# Guessing method names without discovery
bdg cdp Page.screenshot 2>&1  # FAILS - wrong method name
bdg cdp Screenshot 2>&1       # FAILS - wrong domain
bdg cdp capture 2>&1          # FAILS - incomplete name

# Results in: "Error: Method not found"
# Wastes time with trial and error
```

✅ **AFTER** (Correct - Progressive disclosure):
```bash
# Step 1: List all domains
bdg cdp --list  # → Discover "Page" domain exists

**Validation**: `domains_discovered`

# Step 2: Explore specific domain
bdg cdp --describe Page  # → Find "captureScreenshot" method

**Validation**: `method_identified`

# Step 3: Get method details
bdg cdp --describe Page.captureScreenshot  # → See parameters and return type

**Validation**: `signature_understood`

# Step 4: Execute discovered method
bdg cdp Page.captureScreenshot 2>&1

**Validation**: `execution_successful`
```

**Why better**: No guessing, documentation always current, clear understanding before execution, eliminates trial-and-error.

**Finding Methods by Use Case**:

```bash
# Find cookie-related methods
bdg cdp --search cookie

# Find performance methods
bdg cdp --search performance

# Find DOM manipulation
bdg cdp --search DOM

# Find network interception
bdg cdp --search intercept
```

---

## 8. ADVANCED PATTERNS

### Multi-Session Management

```bash
# Session 1: Monitor network
bdg https://example.com 2>&1 &
SESSION_1_PID=$!

# Session 2: Different URL
bdg https://test.com 2>&1 &
SESSION_2_PID=$!

# Check both sessions
bdg status 2>&1

# Stop specific session (requires manual intervention or session IDs)
kill $SESSION_1_PID
kill $SESSION_2_PID
```

### Conditional Execution

```bash
# Execute CDP only if session active
if bdg status 2>&1 | grep -q "active"; then
  bdg cdp Page.captureScreenshot 2>&1
else
  echo "No active session"
fi

# Retry with exponential backoff
for i in {1..5}; do
  bdg https://example.com 2>&1 && break
  echo "Retry $i failed, waiting..."
  sleep $((2**i))
done
```

### Data Extraction Pipelines

```bash
# Extract, transform, load pattern
bdg https://example.com 2>&1 && \
bdg dom eval "document.querySelectorAll('a')" 2>&1 | \
jq -r '.result.value[] | .href' | \
sort -u > links.txt && \
bdg stop 2>&1

# Performance monitoring pipeline
bdg https://example.com 2>&1 && \
bdg cdp Performance.enable 2>&1 && \
sleep 5 && \
bdg cdp Performance.getMetrics 2>&1 | \
jq '.result.metrics[] | select(.name | contains("DOM"))' > perf.json && \
bdg stop 2>&1
```

---

## 9. BEST PRACTICES

1. **Always verify session status** before executing CDP commands
2. **Use helpers when available** (simpler than raw CDP)
3. **Capture stderr** with `2>&1` for comprehensive error handling
4. **Use jq for JSON processing** instead of string manipulation
5. **Clean up sessions** with `bdg stop` after completion
6. **Progressive disclosure** - start with --describe before executing
7. **Case-insensitive method names** - bdg normalizes automatically
8. **Semantic exit codes** - check `$?` for scripting

---

5: 
6: # Advanced Session Management
7: 
8: Advanced patterns for session lifecycle, multi-session handling, and state persistence.
9: 
10: ---
11: 
12: ## 1. OVERVIEW
13: 
14: Always verify session state before operations - session failures cascade. Implement proper cleanup patterns (trap-based) to prevent resource leaks and orphaned browser processes.
15: 
16: ---
17: 
18: ## 2. PREREQUISITES
19: 
20: **Foundation:** Follow session basics from SKILL.md Section 3 before using advanced patterns:
21: - **Session Lifecycle**: Start → Verify → Execute → Stop workflow
22: - **Session States**: inactive, starting, active, error
23: - **Basic Verification**: `bdg status 2>&1 | jq '.state'`
24: - See [../SKILL.md](../SKILL.md) Section 3 "HOW IT WORKS"
25: 
26: **Recommended Tools**:
27: - `jq` for JSON parsing (install: `brew install jq` or `apt-get install jq`)
28: - Bash 4.0+ for array support and trap handling
29: 
30: ---
31: 
32: ## 3. SESSION LIFECYCLE
33: 
34: ### Basic Lifecycle Pattern
35: 
36: ```bash
37: # 1. Start session
38: bdg <url>
39: 
40: # 2. Verify session active
41: bdg status
42: 
43: # 3. Execute operations
44: bdg cdp <Method>
45: bdg dom screenshot output.png
46: bdg console --list
47: 
48: # 4. Stop session
49: bdg stop
50: ```
51: 
52: **Session States**:
53: - `inactive` - No browser connection
54: - `starting` - Browser launching
55: - `active` - Ready for commands
56: - `error` - Connection failed
57: 
58: ---
59: 
60: ## 4. SESSION START PATTERNS
61: 
62: ### Standard Start
63: 
64: ```bash
65: # Start with URL
66: bdg https://example.com 2>&1
67: 
68: # Check if started successfully
69: if bdg status 2>&1 | grep -q "active"; then
70:   echo "Session ready"
71: else
72:   echo "Session failed to start"
73:   exit 1
74: fi
75: ```
76: 
77: ### Start with Retry Logic
78: 
79: ```bash
80: #!/bin/bash
81: # Robust session start with retries
82: 
83: MAX_RETRIES=3
84: RETRY_DELAY=2
85: 
86: start_session() {
87:   local url=$1
88:   local attempt=1
89: 
90:   while [ $attempt -le $MAX_RETRIES ]; do
91:     echo "Attempt $attempt: Starting session for $url"
92: 
93:     bdg "$url" 2>&1
94:     sleep $RETRY_DELAY
95: 
96:     if bdg status 2>&1 | grep -q "active"; then
97:       echo "Session started successfully"
98:       return 0
99:     fi
100: 
101:     echo "Retry $attempt failed"
102:     attempt=$((attempt + 1))
103:     sleep $((RETRY_DELAY * attempt))
104:   done
105: 
106:   echo "Failed to start session after $MAX_RETRIES attempts"
107:   return 1
108: }
109: 
110: start_session "https://example.com"
111: ```
112: 
113: ### Start with Timeout
114: 
115: ```bash
116: #!/bin/bash
117: # Session start with timeout
118: 
119: SESSION_TIMEOUT=30
120: 
121: start_with_timeout() {
122:   local url=$1
123: 
124:   # Start in background
125:   bdg "$url" 2>&1 &
126:   local pid=$!
127: 
128:   # Wait with timeout
129:   local elapsed=0
130:   while [ $elapsed -lt $SESSION_TIMEOUT ]; do
131:     if bdg status 2>&1 | grep -q "active"; then
132:       echo "Session active after ${elapsed}s"
133:       return 0
134:     fi
135:     sleep 1
136:     elapsed=$((elapsed + 1))
137:   done
138: 
139:   echo "Session timeout after ${SESSION_TIMEOUT}s"
140:   kill $pid 2>/dev/null
141:   return 1
142: }
143: 
144: start_with_timeout "https://example.com"
145: ```
146: 
147: ---
148: 
149: ## 5. SESSION STATUS VERIFICATION
150: 
151: ### Status Check Patterns
152: 
153: ```bash
154: # Simple status check
155: bdg status 2>&1
156: 
157: # Check and extract state
158: session_state=$(bdg status 2>&1 | jq -r '.state')
159: echo "Current state: $session_state"
160: 
161: # Conditional execution based on status
162: if bdg status 2>&1 | jq -e '.state == "active"' > /dev/null; then
163:   bdg dom screenshot output.png 2>&1
164: else
165:   echo "Session not active, cannot capture screenshot"
166: fi
167: ```
168: 
169: ### Health Check Function
170: 
171: ```bash
172: #!/bin/bash
173: # Comprehensive session health check
174: 
175: check_session_health() {
176:   # Check if bdg is installed
177:   command -v bdg >/dev/null 2>&1 || {
178:     echo "ERROR: bdg not installed"
179:     echo "Install: npm install -g browser-debugger-cli@alpha"
180:     return 1
181:   }
182: 
183:   # Check session status
184:   local status_output=$(bdg status 2>&1)
185:   local exit_code=$?
186: 
187:   if [ $exit_code -ne 0 ]; then
188:     echo "ERROR: Status check failed"
189:     echo "$status_output"
190:     return 1
191:   fi
192: 
193:   # Parse status
194:   local state=$(echo "$status_output" | jq -r '.state // "unknown"')
195: 
196:   case "$state" in
197:     active)
198:       echo "✓ Session healthy and active"
199:       return 0
200:       ;;
201:     starting)
202:       echo "⚠ Session starting..."
203:       return 2
204:       ;;
205:     inactive)
206:       echo "✗ No active session"
207:       return 3
208:       ;;
209:     error)
210:       echo "✗ Session in error state"
211:       echo "$status_output" | jq '.error // "Unknown error"'
212:       return 4
213:       ;;
214:     *)
215:       echo "✗ Unknown session state: $state"
216:       return 5
217:       ;;
218:   esac
219: }
220: 
221: check_session_health
222: ```
223: 
224: ---
225: 
226: ## 6. MULTI-SESSION MANAGEMENT
227: 
228: ### Concurrent Sessions Pattern
229: 
230: ```bash
231: #!/bin/bash
232: # Manage multiple browser sessions concurrently
233: 
234: # Session tracking
235: declare -A SESSIONS
236: 
237: start_tracked_session() {
238:   local name=$1
239:   local url=$2
240: 
241:   echo "Starting session: $name"
242:   bdg "$url" 2>&1
243: 
244:   # Store session metadata
245:   SESSIONS[$name]="$url"
246: 
247:   # Verify
248:   if bdg status 2>&1 | grep -q "active"; then
249:     echo "✓ Session $name active"
250:   else
251:     echo "✗ Session $name failed"
252:     unset SESSIONS[$name]
253:   fi
254: }
255: 
256: stop_tracked_session() {
257:   local name=$1
258: 
259:   if [ -n "${SESSIONS[$name]}" ]; then
260:     echo "Stopping session: $name"
261:     bdg stop 2>&1
262:     unset SESSIONS[$name]
263:   else
264:     echo "No session found: $name"
265:   fi
266: }
267: 
268: list_sessions() {
269:   echo "Active sessions:"
270:   for name in "${!SESSIONS[@]}"; do
271:     echo "  - $name: ${SESSIONS[$name]}"
272:   done
273: }
274: 
275: # Usage
276: start_tracked_session "production" "https://example.com"
277: start_tracked_session "staging" "https://staging.example.com"
278: list_sessions
279: stop_tracked_session "production"
280: stop_tracked_session "staging"
281: ```
282: 
283: ### Sequential Session Processing
284: 
285: ```bash
286: #!/bin/bash
287: # Process multiple URLs sequentially with session cleanup
288: 
289: urls=(
290:   "https://example.com"
291:   "https://test.com"
292:   "https://demo.com"
293: )
294: 
295: process_url() {
296:   local url=$1
297:   local filename=$(echo "$url" | sed 's|https://||' | sed 's|/|-|g')
298: 
299:   echo "Processing: $url"
300: 
301:   # Start session
302:   bdg "$url" 2>&1 || return 1
303: 
304:   # Wait for page load
305:   sleep 3
306: 
307:   # Capture data
308:   bdg dom screenshot "${filename}.png" 2>&1
309:   bdg console --list 2>&1 > "${filename}-console.json"
310:   bdg network har "${filename}.har" 2>&1
311: 
312:   # Cleanup
313:   bdg stop 2>&1
314: 
315:   echo "✓ Completed: $url"
316: }
317: 
318: # Process each URL
319: for url in "${urls[@]}"; do
320:   process_url "$url" || echo "Failed: $url"
321:   sleep 2  # Cool-down between sessions
322: done
323: ```
